title: openInFinder: add absolute-path validation mirroring openInApp
description: Fix ISS-001: the openInFinder tRPC endpoint is a publicProcedure with input z.string() and no path validation — it passes input directly to shell.showItemInFolder. The sibling openInApp endpoint (line 163) already validates nodePath.isAbsolute. Add the same guard to openInFinder and delete the bare TODO comment.
action: In apps/desktop/src/lib/trpc/routers/external/index.ts openInFinder mutation (lines 143-148): replace the `// Phase 4: 添加路径验证 — 检查绝对路径、规范化路径、防止路径遍历` comment + `shell.showItemInFolder(input)` with a guard that throws TRPCError code BAD_REQUEST when `!nodePath.isAbsolute(input)`, then calls `shell.showItemInFolder(input)`. Mirror openInApp's exact error message shape: `openInFinder requires an absolute path (got ${JSON.stringify(input)}).` Reuse the existing `nodePath` import (line 2) and `TRPCError` import (line 9) — no new imports needed.

criteria: grep -n 'isAbsolute' apps/desktop/src/lib/trpc/routers/external/index.ts returns at least 2 matches (openInApp line 163 + new openInFinder guard)
! grep -n 'Phase 4: 添加路径验证' apps/desktop/src/lib/trpc/routers/external/index.ts returns no matches (TODO comment deleted)
grep -n 'openInFinder requires an absolute path' apps/desktop/src/lib/trpc/routers/external/index.ts returns one match

grep -n 'code: "BAD_REQUEST"' apps/desktop/src/lib/trpc/routers/external/index.ts returns at least 4 matches (existing 3: withResolveGuard line 37 + openUrl line 122 + openInApp line 165, plus the new openInFinder guard = 4 total)
cd apps/desktop && bun run typecheck exits 0
convergence.verification: Run grep checks to confirm the isAbsolute guard + error message + TODO removal, then `cd apps/desktop && bun run typecheck` exits 0. Optional manual: call openInFinder with a relative path and confirm a BAD_REQUEST error.
convergence.definition_of_done: openInFinder rejects relative paths with a clear BAD_REQUEST error, matching openInApp's validation contract — no unvalidated input reaches shell.showItemInFolder.

files.change: Replace the comment + `shell.showItemInFolder(input)` with: `if (!nodePath.isAbsolute(input)) { throw new TRPCError({ code: "BAD_REQUEST", message: \`openInFinder requires an absolute path (got ${JSON.stringify(input)}).\` }); } shell.showItemInFolder(input);`

implementation: Step 1: Read external/index.ts to confirm openInFinder is at lines 143-148 and openInApp's guard pattern at lines 163-168.

Step 2: Edit the openInFinder mutation — delete the `// Phase 4: 添加路径验证 — 检查绝对路径、规范化路径、防止路径遍历` comment line, insert the isAbsolute guard before `shell.showItemInFolder(input)`, mirroring openInApp's TRPCError shape with message prefix `openInFinder requires an absolute path`.
Step 3: Confirm `nodePath` (line 2) and `TRPCError` (line 9) imports already exist — no import changes needed.
Step 4: Run `cd apps/desktop && bun run typecheck` and the convergence grep checks.

reference.pattern: Mirror openInApp's absolute-path guard (lines 163-168 of the same file) — identical TRPCError BAD_REQUEST + JSON.stringify(input) message shape.
reference.examples: openInApp guard: `if (!nodePath.isAbsolute(input.path)) { throw new TRPCError({ code: "BAD_REQUEST", message: \`openInApp requires an absolute path (got ${JSON.stringify(input.path)}).\` }); }`

rationale.chosen_approach: Inline guard mirroring openInApp rather than extracting a shared helper — only 2 call sites and the inputs differ (openInApp validates input.path, openInFinder validates input directly). A shared assertAbsolutePath helper would add indirection for negligible DRY benefit; the review finding explicitly recommends reusing the isAbsolute check pattern, which this does.
decision_factors: openInFinder input is a bare z.string(); openInApp input is z.object({path, ...}) — different shapes.
shell.showItemInFolder reveals a path in the OS file manager — relative input would resolve against Electron's cwd, surfacing wrong files.
rationale.tradeoffs: Slight duplication of the isAbsolute guard (2 sites) vs. a helper; duplication is safer here given different input shapes and only 2 sites.

risks.risk: Frontend callers passing relative paths to openInFinder break
risks.mitigation: Behavior change is intentional (security fix); callers should pass absolute paths. Verify no renderer call site passes a relative path via grep before/after.
